title: 🚨 Deploy failure: Local CI failed — deploy blocked
body:
## Deploy Blocker — 2026-03-29 01:05 PDT

CI pipeline blocked the deploy.

```
  ✅ index.html has title   ✅ index.html has root div   ✅ JS bundle built   ✅ Manifest built   ✅ SW built   ✅ Server responds 200 (HTTP 200)   ✅ Assets reachable (HTTP 200) └─ ✅ Server Smoke Test passed   ❌  Unit Tests   ✅  TypeScript + Build 
```

Check `bot/ci.log` for full details. Fix the failing tests or build before the next deploy.

---
*Auto-filed by deploy.sh — pick up and investigate.*
enhanced_description:
Deploy pipeline (deploy.sh) ran local CI and unit tests failed, blocking deployment.

- What the user reported: The automated deploy pipeline detected unit test failures and blocked the deploy. All other checks passed (HTML structure, JS bundle, manifest, service worker, server smoke test, TypeScript build).
- What component/area is affected: CI pipeline (bot/ci.log), unit tests, and potentially app source code if tests are failing due to recent changes.
- Expected behaviour: All unit tests pass and deploy proceeds.
- Current behaviour: Unit tests fail, deploy is blocked.
- Edge cases: The test failures may be flaky tests, missing test fixtures, or genuine regressions from recent code changes.
- Acceptance criteria: Identify the failing test(s) from bot/ci.log, fix the root cause (test or source code), and verify all tests pass.
- Investigation steps: Read bot/ci.log to identify which tests fail and why, then apply the appropriate fix.
implementation_notes:
## Investigation Results

The CI failure that triggered this auto-filed issue has already been resolved.

- **ci.log analysis:** The current bot/ci.log shows ALL 291 unit tests passing, TypeScript + Build passing, and Server Smoke Test passing. Final verdict: "All CI stages passed — safe to deploy."
- **Root cause:** The deploy failure was transient — a previous CI run failed (unit tests), but a subsequent successful deploy (commit 908f3f3) overwrote the ci.log with passing results.
- **Action taken:** No code changes needed. The issue is stale — the blocker no longer exists.
- **Verification:** git log confirms successful auto-deploys after this issue was filed (908f3f3).
- **Files changed:** None — the issue resolved itself via subsequent successful CI runs.

title: readme is not updated
body:
## 🐛 Bug Report

readme is not updated

---

**Category:** `bug`
**Source:** Telegram Bot
**Status:** Pending implementation

> _This issue was created automatically via the BabyBloom Telegram Bot. No private baby data is included._
enhanced_description:
The project README needs to be updated to reflect the current state of the application.

- What the user reported: The README is not updated — it likely does not reflect recent features, setup instructions, or architecture changes.
- What component/area is affected: README.md at the project root.
- Expected behaviour: README accurately describes the app features, setup instructions, tech stack, and current capabilities.
- Current behaviour: README is outdated or incomplete.
- Edge cases: Ensure no private data or secrets are included in the README.
- Acceptance criteria: README.md is updated to reflect the current app features, architecture, setup instructions, and any recent changes. It should be clear and helpful for new contributors or users.
implementation_notes:
## Changes Made
- Files changed: README.md
- Added Mom Wellness section (tracks hydration, meals, vitamins, sleep quality, mood)
- Added Hero Widget section (customizable backgrounds, last activity times, age-based reminders)
- Added Welcome Experience section (auto-animated onboarding carousel)
- Updated diaper tracking from "wet/dirty" to "pee/poop" terminology
- Updated partner sync to mention QR code support alongside copy/paste
- Added Capacitor 6 to tech stack table (iOS & Android native builds)
- Added wellness/ to project structure under features/
- Added mobile build scripts (build:mobile, mobile:ios, mobile:android) to available scripts
- Fixed pipeline timing from "every 30 minutes" to "every hour"
- Updated privacy section to mention QR codes for sync

title: the log and other tabs other than home doesn’t follow the UX experience as of...
body:
## ✨ Feature Request

the log and other tabs other than home doesn’t follow the UX experience as of home. It’s little out dated

---

**Category:** `enhancement`
**Source:** Telegram Bot
**Status:** Pending implementation

> _This issue was created automatically via the BabyBloom Telegram Bot. No private baby data is included._
enhanced_description:
The user reports that the Log tab and other non-Home tabs (likely Growth, Settings, etc.) have an outdated or inconsistent UX compared to the Home tab. The Home tab has been updated with a modern look and feel, but the other tabs have not been brought in line.

What needs to happen:
- Audit all non-Home tabs (Log, Growth, Settings, etc.) and identify UX/styling differences vs Home
- Update layout, spacing, typography, card styles, color usage, and interaction patterns to match Home
- Ensure consistent header/navigation styling across all tabs
- Maintain each tab's unique functionality while harmonizing the visual design language

Components likely affected:
- Log/Feed tracking views
- Growth chart views
- Settings/profile views
- Any other tab-based views

Acceptance criteria:
- All tabs share the same design language as the Home tab (consistent spacing, typography, card styles, colors)
- No regression in functionality on any tab
- Responsive layout remains intact

Testing:
- Add unit tests verifying that key UI components render correctly after styling updates
- Add snapshot or visual regression tests if the project supports them
- Manual verification that all tabs look consistent with Home
implementation_notes:
UX consistency update — align non-Home tabs with Home tab design language.

Files changed:
- NEW: src/components/shared/TabHeader.tsx — gradient banner header component matching Home hero style (gradient bg, decorative orbs, glassmorphic icon, white text)
- src/components/shared/index.ts — export new TabHeader component
- src/tabs/LogTab.tsx — replaced plain SectionHeader with TabHeader, added card-styled date navigator, colored left-border accent on log entries
- src/tabs/GuideTab.tsx — replaced SectionHeader with TabHeader, added box-shadow and transitions to accordion headers
- src/tabs/MilestonesTab.tsx — replaced SectionHeader with TabHeader, added box-shadow and transitions to accordion headers
- src/tabs/SafetyTab.tsx — replaced SectionHeader with TabHeader, added box-shadow and transitions to accordion headers

Tests added:
- tests/unit/tabHeader.test.ts — 17 test cases covering TabHeader component, shared exports, all tabs using TabHeader, accordion styling, and LogTab improvements

Approach:
- Created a shared TabHeader component that mimics the Home tab hero section (gradient background, decorative orbs, glassmorphic elements, white text on gradient)
- Each tab gets its own color scheme that matches its semantic purpose
- Accordion sections across Guide/Milestones/Safety now have elevated card shadows and smooth transitions
- LogTab entries get a colored left border accent for visual timeline effect
- Date navigator in LogTab is now a proper card component

Note: vitest could not run in the sandbox due to esbuild platform mismatch (Mac node_modules in Linux sandbox). Tests were verified by running assertions manually with Node. All 17 pass. Regression tests (node tests/regression.cjs) also pass 21/21.
